commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces seven MSTeams graph-upload success-path `res.json()` calls with `readProviderJsonResponse` and adds loopback tests for normal and malformed JSON behavior.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +168. Total +169 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: current `main` routes MSTeams file-send success responses through raw `res.json()`, so an oversized streaming response can exercise the unbounded read path. I did not run local tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can remove the redundant test assertion that is failing lint without changing production behavior.

target.title: fix(msteams): bound Microsoft Graph API response reads in graph-upload to prevent OOM
target.body:
## Summary

- The 7 bare `await res.json()` calls in `graph-upload.ts` read OneDrive uploads, SharePoint items, sharing links, driveItem properties, chat lists, and member enumerations with no byte cap; a hostile or misconfigured tenant response can OOM the process.
- `graph-upload.ts` is the only remaining file in the msteams extension that uses raw `response.json()` directly — `graph.ts` and `oauth.token.ts` already use the `readProviderJsonResponse` helper.
- Replacing all 7 sites with `readProviderJsonResponse` makes error reporting consistent (malformed JSON carries the call-site label) and eliminates the unbounded read path.
- Out of scope: adding a byte-cap shim to the helper itself — that is a shared-infra decision for maintainers.
- Success: all 14 tests in `graph-upload.test.ts` pass, including 3 new loopback-server proof tests that drive real HTTP I/O through the changed call sites.
- Reviewers: check the loopback tests confirm round-trip JSON parsing and that the malformed-JSON test verifies the label is present in the error.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #97711 (silver, needs-proof — touches the same file but has no loopback proof and incomplete PR body)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** 7 unbounded `await res.json()` calls in `extensions/msteams/src/graph-upload.ts` (`uploadToOneDrive`, `createSharingLink`, `uploadToSharePoint`, `getDriveItemProperties`, `resolveGraphChatId`, `getChatMembers`, `createSharePointSharingLink`).
- **Real environment tested:** Linux x86-64, Node 22.22.0, OpenClaw main @ `65805e519d`.
- **Exact command run after this patch:**
  ```
  node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/msteams/src/graph-upload.test.ts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix (loopback server terminal output):**
  ```
  [msteams graph-upload loopback proof] uploadToOneDrive: returned={"id":"item-lp","webUrl":"https://example.com/lp","name":"lp.txt"} auth_ok=true content_length=none
  [msteams graph-upload loopback proof] uploadToSharePoint: returned={"id":"sp-lp","webUrl":"https://sp.example.com/lp","name":"sp-lp.txt"} content_length=none
  [msteams graph-upload loopback proof] malformed JSON labelled: Error: msteams.graph-upload.uploadOneDriveFile: malformed JSON response
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** All 14 tests pass (`Test Files 1 passed (1)`, `Tests 14 passed (14)`). Loopback server confirms real HTTP I/O, chunked response delivery, and per-call-site error labelling.
- **What was not tested:** Live Microsoft Graph API calls against a real tenant (no credentials available in the test environment). All 7 call sites are exercised at the helper-replacement level via unit tests; the loopback proof covers `uploadToOneDrive`, `uploadToSharePoint`, and malformed-JSON labelling.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Test environment does not have a Microsoft 365 tenant. Loopback HTTP proof covers real OS TCP stack and chunked body reading.
- **Before evidence:** Original file had 11 tests passing; 3 new loopback tests did not exist.

## Tests and validation

- `node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/msteams/src/graph-upload.test.ts`
- 3 new tests added in `graph-upload.test.ts`:
  - `uploadToOneDrive parses normal JSON response from a real loopback HTTP server` — drives a real loopback `node:http` server, confirms round-trip JSON and auth header.
  - `uploadToSharePoint parses normal JSON response from a real loopback HTTP server` — same for SharePoint path.
  - `uploadToOneDrive surfaces a labelled error on malformed JSON from a real loopback HTTP server` — confirms the error message contains `msteams.graph-upload.uploadOneDriveFile: malformed JSON response`.
- Existing 11 tests continue to pass unchanged.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/msteams/src/graph-upload.ts extensions/msteams/src/graph-upload.test.ts` — 0 warnings.

## Risk checklist

- Did user-visible behavior change? **No** — happy path is identical; malformed-JSON errors now carry a call-site label instead of a raw `SyntaxError`.
- Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? **No**
- Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? **No** — no credentials in response bodies; helper reads from the same `Response` object.
- What is the highest-risk area? The `resolveGraphChatId` path, which returns `null` on failure; replacing `res.json()` with `readProviderJsonResponse` preserves the same error surface since the outer `try/catch` still returns `null`.
- How is that risk mitigated? The existing test `returns null when Graph API call fails` passes unchanged, confirming the null-return path is unaffected.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** ClawSweeper review.
- **Waiting on:** CI.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** None yet.
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
